# Vendored dependencies

Policy: everything under `vendor/` is a pinned, licensed, point-in-time artifact that cannot
(yet) be resolved from a public repository. Each entry documents provenance (source, SHA,
license) and the exact refresh procedure. Replace with normal dependency coordinates the
moment upstream publishes.

This is distinct from `reference/` (gitignored full clones of upstream repos used as porting
oracles) — `vendor/` contents are committed and shipped-adjacent.

## `lib/doris-fe-sql-parser-1.2-SNAPSHOT-g7027772afcb.jar`

The standalone Doris SQL parser (`fe-sql-parser`) from Apache Doris — the authoritative ANTLR
grammar, planned to back the JVM-only native-parser adapter (fragment validation /
engine-exact parsing; see docs/parsing-research-and-plan.md Phase 7). **Pre-release, not
published to any public Maven repository** — Doris publishes connectors/SDKs, not FE core
modules — so we vendor a locally-built jar.

Vendored in coordination with the doris-intellij-plugin project (same artifact, same pin).

| | |
|---|---|
| Source | Apache Doris — https://github.com/apache/doris, module `fe/fe-sql-parser` |
| Git SHA | `7027772afcbf36972662ad0c71dfc9f47bb13f4e` (short `g7027772afcb`) |
| `git describe` | `v20220306-27095-g7027772afcb` |
| Commit date | 2026-07-02 |
| Artifact version | `1.2-SNAPSHOT` (internal Doris build version — NOT a stable coordinate) |
| License | Apache License 2.0 (see ATTRIBUTIONS.md at repo root) |
| Runtime dependency | `org.antlr:antlr4-runtime:4.13.1` (from Maven Central, not bundled) |

### Rebuilding / refreshing

From a Doris checkout, JDK 17 (FE targets 17):

```bash
JAVA_HOME=/path/to/jdk-17 \
  mvn -f fe/pom.xml -pl fe-sql-parser -am -Pflatten install -DskipTests
# output: fe/fe-sql-parser/target/doris-fe-sql-parser.jar (~1.3 MB)
cp fe/fe-sql-parser/target/doris-fe-sql-parser.jar \
   vendor/lib/doris-fe-sql-parser-1.2-SNAPSHOT-g<short-sha>.jar
```

> Replace with a Maven coordinate once Doris publishes `fe-sql-parser` publicly —
> **expected on Maven Central within weeks of 2026-07** (coordinated with the Doris team).
> When it lands: swap the module dependency to the coordinate, delete `vendor/lib/`, and
> update ATTRIBUTIONS.md + this file.

## Doris function registry (extracted data, not a binary)

The Doris function catalog (`brikk-sql-metadata/.../GeneratedDorisFunctionCatalog.kt`) is
generated by `tools/generate_doris_functions.py` from Doris's runtime function registry
(`fe/fe-core/.../catalog/Builtin*Functions.java` in the pinned `reference/doris` checkout) —
the same source of truth Doris registers at startup, more authoritative than docs. Regenerate
against a newer checkout to refresh. Signatures (arg/return types) are not yet extracted —
they live on each function class's static `SIGNATURES` field and require building `fe-core`
(planned: signature-dumper pass; the catalog model is overload-ready).

## DuckDB function registry (extracted data, not a binary)

The DuckDB catalog (`brikk-sql-metadata/.../GeneratedDuckdbFunctionCatalog.kt`) is generated
by `tools/generate_duckdb_functions.py` from the embedded engine itself: the python `duckdb`
module's `duckdb_functions()` view — the engine's own resolved registry, with per-overload
signatures and varargs included for free. Pinned to the python module version at generation
time (currently **v1.5.4**; the generator refuses to run if module and engine versions
disagree). Refresh: bump the python `duckdb` module, re-run the script. DuckDB is MIT
licensed (see ATTRIBUTIONS.md).

## `data/trino-functions-481.tsv`

Trino **481**'s complete built-in function registry (`SHOW FUNCTIONS`), one row per
overload, 6 tab-separated columns: name, return type, argument types, function type,
deterministic, description. 746 rows / 319 distinct names (597 scalar, 129 aggregate,
16 window, 4 table).

| | |
|---|---|
| Source | Trino — https://github.com/trinodb/trino |
| Version | 481 (Maven Central `io.trino:trino-main:481`) |
| License | Apache License 2.0 (extracted registry data; see ATTRIBUTIONS.md) |

### Generation method

Method B of trino-ducklake's `GENERATE-TRINO-FUNCTION-CATALOG.md` recipe: a throwaway
Gradle project with `io.trino:trino-main:481` + `io.trino:trino-testing:481` + JUnit 5 on
the test classpath (JDK 25; JVM flags `--add-modules jdk.incubator.vector` plus the usual
`--add-opens`), booting an in-JVM `DistributedQueryRunner` (session catalog `system`,
schema `runtime`) and dumping `SHOW FUNCTIONS` to TSV. Version-pinned and reproducible —
no external Trino server involved. Refresh on a version bump: re-run the harness against
the new pin, save as `data/trino-functions-<version>.tsv`, then re-run
`tools/generate_trino_functions.py` (TSV -> `GeneratedTrinoFunctionCatalog.kt`).

Known caveats (carried into the generated Kotlin header): `SHOW FUNCTIONS` does not flag
variadics (arity is a lower bound), parametric types / type variables appear literally
(`varchar(x)`, `decimal(p,s)`, `E`), and operators are grammar-level and absent.
